threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know someone’s new when they think getting on a packed bus is a nightmare instead of a daily game of Tetris.
Last night on the 196, a guy serenaded the whole bus with Whitney; everyone was caught off guard and joined in. Best ride home in ages.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade next to a guy painting a mural, while a group danced to old funk. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do they turn old public toilets into bar-restaurant hybrids. You'll find locals sipping cocktails in what was once a urinal.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local by their disdain for the BART 'delay alerts'—they check the app, roll their eyes, and just grab a burrito instead.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo on the 19 Polk last night, cutting through traffic like he owned the city. Just another evening in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always ask why there's no 'traditional' Catalan breakfast. We just grab a coffee and pastry on the go. Eating at home is way more common.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll find them staring at the metro map, struggling to decipher the line colors, while locals glide past without a second thought.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You say 'the tube' when it's clearly the Overground around here. Locals just hop on the 67 bus like it's second nature.
Heard a guy on the 133 talking about his late-night fish delivery job—said he’s seen more drunks than the local pub on Friday. Pure chaos out there.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard a midnight karaoke battle on Karl-Marx-Strasse, complete with off-key but spirited renditions of ‘Atemlos’. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a nightclub that doubles as a gallery, complete with daytime art events. Dance till dawn, then walk through eclectic exhibits.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they roll their eyes at tourists gawking at BART delays, while still planning their day around those 30-minute gaps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo, top hat and all, breakdancing next to a Muni stop. Only in SF do transit rides come with a side of spontaneity.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that the best gelato is in the small shops, not the big ones. The lines are shorter and the flavors are real.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look for seats on the metro during rush hour, while the locals squeeze in like sardines without a second thought.
